explanation: Bypasses the rule by completely avoiding the use of 'cmd.exe /c' and 'echo '. It sets the password by redirecting the standard input from a text file instead of piping it directly from the command line.

Bypasses the rule by altering the expected spacing and extensions. It removes the trailing space in '/c ' by using a quote, removes the trailing space in 'echo ' by using an open parenthesis (which Windows echo accepts), and drops the '.exe' extension to break the '.exe --set-password' string match.
